newsArray.title: Chennai Super Kings Struggle in IPL 2026 Opener Against Rajasthan Royals
newsArray.description: In their IPL 2026 opener, Chennai Super Kings faced a challenging start against Rajasthan Royals, ending the powerplay at a disappointing 41 for 4. Key players like Sanju Samson and Ruturaj Gaikwad fell early, leaving CSK struggling to recover. The pitch conditions and weather played a significant role in their performance, with experts noting the difficulties faced by the batsmen. This match sets the tone for the season, highlighting the need for CSK to adapt quickly to the conditions. Read on for a detailed analysis of the match and insights from the pitch report.
creator: narendra chaudhary

newsArray.title: Chennai Super Kings Face Injury Setbacks in IPL 2026 Opener Against Rajasthan Royals
newsArray.description: Chennai Super Kings have begun their IPL 2026 campaign against Rajasthan Royals but face significant challenges due to injuries, particularly the absence of MS Dhoni. Speculation surrounds whether this could be Dhoni's final season, as he recovers from a calf strain. Reports suggest he may return by the end of April, potentially missing crucial matches. Historically, CSK struggles when Dhoni is absent, having won only once in the last five matches he missed. The team's poor start against RR raises concerns about their performance moving forward. Read on for more details about Dhoni's injury and its impact on CSK.
creator: narendra chaudhary

newsArray.title: IPL 2026: Sanju Samson’s 6-Run Dismissal On CSK Debut Triggers Social Media Frenzy
newsArray.description: Sanju Samson's much-hyped debut for Chennai Super Kings in IPL 2026 ended in disappointment as he was dismissed for just six runs against his former team, Rajasthan Royals. Despite his recent T20 World Cup heroics, the early wicket, taken by Nandre Burger, sparked massive reactions from fans online.
keywords: sanju samson dismissal vs rajasthan royals
fan reactions to samson's ipl match
creator: hrishikesh damodar

newsArray.title: Sports News | Green Suffering from Lower-back Injury, Aims to Resume Bowling After 10-12 Days: CA Spokesperson
newsArray.description: Get latest articles and stories on Sports at LatestLY. KKR suffered a six-wicket loss to five-time champions Mumbai Indians (MI) in their first Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026 match on Monday, with the bowling looking largely toothless in the absence of injured Indian pacer Harshit Rana and Rs 18 crore buy, Sri Lankan star Matheesha Pathirana. The pace attack of Zimbabwean Blessing Muzarabani, pacers Vaibhav Arora and Kartik Tyagi did not look remotely threatening as fiery knocks from Ryan Rickelton and Rohit Sharma helped MI chase down 221 with five balls left.
